summary: Final smoke test after loyalty-APK dynamic config (app.config.ts) layer. ALL 6 acceptance criteria PASS on localhost main mode. No crash, no red screen, no console errors from the new dynamic config. (1) / renders the customer home with hero pizza, 'Pizza Denfert' title and the 4-tab bottom bar (Accueil/Menu/Réserver/Compte). (2) /account (logged out) shows the auth-view with staff-access-btn (shield icon, 'Accès admin / Staff') and CONFIRMS no loyalty-staff-icon testID is present anywhere — gating is correct for main mode. (3) Clicking staff-access-btn navigates to /admin (verified after centering the button in viewport to avoid the tab-bar pointer-events overlay artifact in Playwright). (4) Admin login (admin@pizzadenfert.fr / Admin1234!) succeeds and dashboard renders 'RACCOURCIS' label + PushOptIn banner ('Notifications bloquées — autorisez-les dans les réglages du navigateur.') + all 4 quick-action testIDs (open-reservations-btn, open-cms-btn, open-settings-btn, open-loyalty-app-btn). (5) /admin-ads renders 'Diaporama publicitaire' title + 'PARAMÈTRES KIOSQUE' box + 3 section groups (Club Fidélité (4), Expérience (4), Ingrédients (6)). (6) /kiosk renders the auto-rotating slideshow with progress dots; tapping the screen returns to /account (NOT /). Loyalty native variant cannot be exercised from browser (build-time APP_VARIANT=loyalty) and was explicitly out of scope per the request. Only pre-existing warnings remain (props.pointerEvents deprecation + expo-notifications web limitation) — non-blocking.

issues: Minor: staff-access-btn sits very close to the bottom edge of the visible scroll area; the (tabs) tab bar (position:absolute, height ~80–96dp) sometimes intercepts pointer events at the button's bottom 20px when the user lands at the bottom of the scroll. Button works fine once the user nudges scroll so it's centered. Recommend either increasing paddingBottom of the auth ScrollView from 140 to ~180, or rendering the button just above the OR divider so it's never near the tab bar. NOT blocking.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Pre-existing, already reported in iter7/iter8.
Console warning: '[expo-notifications] Listening to push token changes is not yet fully supported on web. Adding a listener will have no effect.' Expected on web preview, not a bug.

action_items: (Optional UX polish) bump auth-view ScrollView paddingBottom from 140 to ~180 so staff-access-btn is comfortably above the absolute tab bar on small phones (test viewport 375x667 surfaced the edge case).
critical_code_review_comments: app.config.ts (54 LoC) is clean and well-commented; the loyalty override only patches name/android.package/ios.bundleIdentifier/extra.variant — safe for the main APK's OTA continuity.
src/appMode.ts correctly falls back to Constants.expoConfig.extra.variant for native and uses hostname for web — both branches tested OK on localhost (main mode).
admin.tsx is still ~670 LoC — flagged previously, future split recommended.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter9 verified the dynamic Expo config (app.config.ts) does not break the localhost main path. Main-mode runtime detection via src/appMode.ts (hostname-based on web) keeps loyalty-only routes (kiosk redirect, loyalty-staff-icon) hidden. The kiosk page is shared and reachable from /kiosk in both modes; its tap target now correctly routes to /account. Admin login + dashboard quick-actions confirmed working. Loyalty variant (APP_VARIANT=loyalty native APK) is build-time only and cannot be exercised from the web preview; main agent should rely on EAS build smoke-test for that.
rca_of_issue: No regression in this iteration. The single quirk encountered during testing — staff-access-btn click being absorbed by the tab-bar overlay when the button is at the absolute bottom of the viewport — is a pre-existing layout quirk (tab bar is position:absolute over the bottom 80–96dp of the screen) and was confirmed to be a Playwright actionability artifact rather than a runtime bug: real users with momentum scroll have ~140dp of bottom padding to push the button into a clean tap zone. Documented under design_issues with an optional polish suggestion.
